title: Endpoint, Mobile, Browser, Wireless, and SOHO Hardening · Changed-Device Lab
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of endpoint, mobile, browser, wireless, and soho hardening into a new layered endpoint hardening and exception register. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layered endpoint hardening and exception register with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.
Choose supported personal or enterprise wireless authentication, encryption, credentials, certificates, onboarding, rotation, revocation, and guest isolation.
Apply screen lock, supported updates, encryption, secure enrollment, app sources, permissions, backups, remote response, connectivity controls, and privacy-aware management.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Hardening Baseline
steps.instruction:
lab case — Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The lab variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Hardening Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Hardening Baseline, keep Hardening Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

Competency probe: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Hardening Regression.
